text_blocks.text: On April 30, 2000, as a result of a
carriage dispute with ABC, Time Warner Cable
the cable provider's systems in four markets
(WABC-TV in New York City, KABC- TV in
Los Angeles, KTRK in Houston and WTVD in
Raleigh-Durham). The network had earlier reached
an eleventh-hour deal to renew its carriage
agreement with the provider on December 31,
1999. ABC filed an emergency petition to the
Federal Communications Commission on May 1 to
force TWC to restore the affected stations; the
FCC ruled in favor of ABC, ordering Time
Warner Cable to restore the stations, doing so
on the afternoon of May 2, ABC ended the
